frameworks.name: ambiguity vs risk decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Choices under known (risky) vs unknown (ambiguous) probabilistic outcomes.

frameworks.name: incidental arousal / stress modulation of value
frameworks.one_line: Tests whether off-task sympathetic arousal or acute stress shifts ambiguity preferences.

core_question: Does incidental sympathetic arousal or acute psychosocial stress bias ambiguity (vs risk) preferences?
key_assumptions: Ambiguity and risk attitudes can be separated within-subject
Pupil dilation and salivary cortisol index successful arousal/stress induction
Prior work linking transient arousal and lifetime stress to ambiguity generalizes to incidental manipulations
manipulations: Acute psychosocial stressor incidental to choice (Experiment 2)
Lottery type: known-probability (risky) vs unknown-probability (ambiguous) vs $5 sure option
key_findings: Participants were more averse to ambiguity than to numerically matched risk
Pupil dilation and cortisol confirmed effective arousal and stress inductions
Neither threat-induced arousal nor acute stress shifted ambiguity preferences
No bias of risk preferences by either manipulation
interpretations: Incidental arousal/stress does not causally modulate ambiguity attitudes, contrary to predictions from prior correlational links
